structure.description: Split feed body into labeled sponsored band (compact, with 'Sponsored' tag per item) followed by organic 'For you' band. Sponsored items never interleave into organic ranking — preserves algorithmic trust.

rationale.hypothesis: Mixing paid and organic placements without visual segregation erodes user trust in the ranking algorithm and violates disclosure norms in commerce/job markets. Explicit segregation lets the platform monetize without contaminating the 'for-you' signal.

evidence.description: Home feed has 'Sponsored Jobs' horizontal strip with tag on each card, separate from 'Jobs for you' vertical list below
'Sponsored' label above ads block, separate from organic results
Sponsored products labeled but interleaved — weaker form of same pattern

counterexample.description: Ads interleaved without structural band — works in infinite-scroll where segregation would break flow rhythm

shouldMatch.reason: Listing.promoted/featured is the canonical sponsor flag

shouldNotMatch.reason: Personal goals — monetization is out of scope
